english: give one's own clothes to a person (e.g. when a king honours any body by arraying him in splendid garments). - mgo-léb (mgo leb) a flat head Cs. - go-šóg (go shog), resp. u-šóg (u shog) cover, of a copy-book etc. Cs. - mgo-srin (mgo srin) n. of a disease Lt.

Ssk. नाथ protector, patron; principal, master, lord; tutelar god; ₒgro-mgón ('gro mgon) protector of beings; skyabs-mgón (skyabs mgon) v. skyabs (skyabs); čìi p̌yír ñai mgon mi byed (ci'i phyir ngai mgon mi byed) why do you not assist me? Dzl.; lhai, bdúd-kyi, gšin-rjei mgón-po (lha'i, bdud kyi, gshin rje'i mgon po) the principal of the gods, of the devils, the lord of death Cs.; mgón-po mčód-pa, stód-pa, rbád-pa (mgon po mchod pa, stod pa, rbad pa) to honour, to praise, the tutelar god, to stir up or urge him to aid one's cause The special tutelar god of Tibet, called mgón-po (mgon po) by preference, is Awalokiteśwara, Spyan-ras-gzígs (spyan ras gzigs); ₒjíg-rten-mgón-po ('jig rten mgon po), or mi-mjéd-žiṅ-gi mgón-po (mi mjed zhing gi mgon po) lord of the world, ₒjíg-rten gsúm-gyi mgón-po ('jig rten gsum gyi mgon po) (Hindi: triloknath), lord or ruler of the three worlds, an epithet 1. of Buddha, 2. of Awalokiteśwara, 3. of the Dharma-Rāja of Bhotān. Comp. mgon máṅs (mgon mangs) many patrons or defenders of religion; many small pyramidical sacred buildings Cs. - mgon-méd (mgon med) unprotected, mgon-med-zas-sbyin (mgon med zas sbyin), अनाथपिण्डद n. of a certain house-owner in Buddha's time, often mentioned in legends.

Ü. *gyóg-po* (*gyog po*) quick, speedy, swift; mgyógs-par (mgyogs par) (seldom mgyógs-la (mgyogs la) Mil.) adv. quickly, speedily, soon; *gyog-riṅ* (*gyog ring*) W speedy, hasty, rash, *gyog-lám* (*gyog lam*) W., C., a straight, short way, a short cut; rkaṅ-mgyógs (rkang mgyogs) v. rkaṅ (rkang). - su-mgyógs (su mgyogs), pronounced *sun-gyóg(s)* (*sun gyog(s)*) W., (lit. 'who is quick?') a race, a racing or running-match.

(Ssk. ग्रीवा) 1. neck, mgrin ríṅ-ba (mgrin ring ba), a long neck, mgrin t'úṅ-ba (mgrin thung ba) a short neck Lt.; mgrin-sṅón (mgrin sngon) blue-necked, an epithet of gods. - 2. throat, as passage or organ of the voice, mgrin gčíg-tu (mgrin gcig tu) (to call as) with one voice, frq.; mgrin-bzáṅ (mgrin bzang) a loud voice Cs.

feast, treat, banquet, entertainment, mgron gtóṅ-ba (mgron gtong ba), resp. sku-mgron ₒbúl-ba (sku mgron 'bul ba) to entertain; *dón-taṅ-k'an* (*don tang khang*) W. host, entertainer; mgrón-la ₒbód-pa (mgron la 'bod pa), resp. mgrón-du spyan-ₒdrén-pa (mgron du spyan 'dren pa), to invite to an entertainment; mgrón-du gnyér-ba (mgron du gnyer ba) to treat, to regale Dzl.; mgrón-du ₒgró-ba (mgron du 'gro ba) to go to an entertainment, a party Dzl. (cf. ₒgrón-du ₒgró-ba ('gron du 'gro ba) to go abroad); zas-mgrón (zas mgron) an entertainment consisting in eating; ǰa-mgrón (ja mgron) a tea-party; čaṅ-mgrón (chang mgron) a treatment with beer or wine Cs.

1. obstruction, stoppage, esp. in comp.: yi-ga-ₒgág (yi ga 'gag) want of appetite; gčìn-ₒgág (gcin 'gag), also -dgag (-dgag), strangury. - 2. a place or spot that has to be passed by all that proceed to a certain point, *zám-pe gág-tu gúg-na kụ́m-ma ₒdzín t'ub* (*zam pe gag tu gug na kụm ma 'dzin thub*) C. the thief may be stopped, if you are on the watch in the thoroughfare of the bridge; rí-bo dpal-bár-gyi ₒgág (ri bo dpal bar gyi 'gag) the place on the Palbár mountain, where there is the only passage Mil.; sgo-ₒgág (sgo 'gag) the door of the house, because through it all that enter or leave have to pass; k'a-ₒgág (kha 'gag) the mouth, through which every thing must pass that is eaten; fig.: t'ar-lám-gyi gnad-ₒgág (thar lam gyi gnad 'gag), the main point for obtaining salvation; ₒgag gčíg-tu dril-ba ('gag gcig tu dril ba) to unite, to be concentrated in one point Mil.

1. vb, (cf. gẹgs-pa (gegs pa)) to stop, to cease, to be at a stand-still; mostly in the perfect form gags (gags); dáṅ-k'a ₒgags (dang kha 'gags) the appetite is gone Mil.; it is also used of the passions having been suppressed, having ceased Mil. - 2. sbst. door-keeper, v. sgo-ₒgág (sgo 'gag) sub ₒgag ('gag).
the burden of an office, business, commission, ₒgan k'úr-ba ('gan khur ba) to bear such a burden, bskúr-ba (bskur ba) to impose it on a person.
= mt'á-ma (mtha' ma), mǰúg-ma (mjug ma), the end, of a bench, a garment etc. Mil.nt.; as postpos. c.genit. after, behind C.
1. Sch.: to take care, to be cautious; orderly, decent. - 2.
